mnemonics: QUEUE THEORY: Breadth-First Search uses a Queue (First-In-First-Out) to explore all neighbors at the present depth before moving deeper.
keyPoints: Explores all neighbor nodes at the present depth prior to moving on to nodes at the next depth level.
Uses a queue data structure (FIFO) to manage the order of node exploration.
Guarantees the shortest path in an unweighted graph.
commonMistakes: Confusing it with Depth-First Search (using a stack instead of a queue).
Forgetting to mark nodes as visited, which can lead to infinite loops in cyclic graphs.
